english: to swallow Med.; kyur-mid-du soṅ-ste (khyur mid du song ste) suffering himself to be swallowed (from the story of an Indian idol) Pth.
wall-side Ts. (?)
1. for kyeu (khye'u) Mil. - 2. for ke (khe) q.v.; Kye-mo (khye mo) v. ke (khe).
n. of a disease Med.
(diminutive of Kyo-bo? (khyo bo?)) 1. male child, infant boy. 2. youth, adolescent B.

pers. pron. 2nd. person, thou, and particularly in the plur. you, in B. eleg., in addressing superiors, but also used by superiors in speaking to inferiors, and even contemptuously: Kyed lta-bui ma-rabs (khyed lta bu'i ma rabs) such vulgar, mean people, as ye are Dzl. -- Kyed-kyi (khyed kyi) thy, your. -- Kyed-raṅ (khyed rang) (Kyed-ñid (khyed nyid) seems to be little used) thou thyself, you yourself; plur. particularly expressed: Kyed-cag (khyed cag), Kyed-rnams (khyed rnams), Kyed-tso (khyed tsho); dge-tsul Kyed ñyis (dge tshul khyed gnyis) you two Getsuls Glr.; Kyed gsum-po (khyed gsum po) you three (a mother speaking to her sons) Glr.; Kyed-cag (khyed cag) you, when speaking to one person Glr., = ñid-cag (nyid cag).
1. = kyid (khyid) W. 2. v. K̥yed-pa ('khyed pa).
Pur. he, she, v. Kun-ti (khun ti).
v. Kebs (khebs).

(Sch. also kem (khem)). a shovel, W.: *kyem daṅ poṅ-ce* (*khyem dang pong ce*) to shovel away, to remove with a shovel; kyem-gyi d̥ab-ma (khyem gyi 'dab ma) the blade of a shovel, yu-ba (yu ba) the handle of it Cs. -- gru-kyem (gru khyem), c'u-kyem (chu khyem) W. oar, lcags-kyem (lcags khyem) spade; me-kyem (me khyem) fire-shovel; wa-kyem (wa khyem) a scoop, hollow gutter-shaped shovel Cs.; kyem-bu (khyem bu) spoon Cs.
v. K̥ye-ba ('khye ba); kyer-so (khyer so) v. k̥yer-so ('khyer so).

B. frq., also kyo-po (khyo po) Pth. 1. man (seldom). 2. husband, Kyo byed-pa (khyo byed pa) ('to act a husband' cf. byed-pa (byed pa) I. 1) to take a wife; kyod ṅai kyo mi byed-na (khyod nga'i khyo mi byed na) if you do not marry me Dzl. -- Kyo-med (khyo med) single, unmarried. -- Kyo-mo (khyo mo) wife Cs. -- Kyo-śug (khyo shug) husband and wife, married couple; Kyo-śug ñyis grogs-nas soṅ (khyo shug gnyis grogs nas song) these two married people went together; Kyo-śug mdza-ba-rnams (khyo shug mdza' ba rnams) a loving couple; kyim-bdag Kyo-śug ñyis (khyim bdag khyo shug gnyis) the citizen with his wife; gser-lha Kyo-śug ñyis (gser lha khyo shug gnyis) about the same as: Mr. and Mrs. Serlha; Kyo-śug-tu sdu-ba (khyo shug tu sdu ba) to join a couple in marriage Dzl.
1. man emphatically, as: skyes-bu ṅa hor-pa yaṅ Kyo-ga yin (skyes bu nga hor pa yang khyo ga yin) we Turks are men, too; hero, Kyo-ga-po (khyo ga po) id. -- 3. heroic deed, exploit.
crooked, curved, bent; Cs. also cunning.
(v. Kyo-ga (khyo ga) and toṅ (thong)) W. young man, youth.
litter, bier Pth., palanquin Cs. also scaffold (?) Cs.
v. k̥yoṅ-ba ('khyong ba).

pers. pron. 2nd. person sing. and plur., thou, you; Kyod-kyi (khyod kyi) thy, thine, your; if plurality is to be especially expressed, it is done by adding cag (cag): Kyod-cag (khyod cag) Mil.; occasionally also Kyod-rnams (khyod rnams), cf. Kyed (khyed); Kyod-raṅ (khyod rang) 1. thou thyself, you yourself. 2. thou, you (W. *Kyo-raṅ* (*khyo rang*)).

size, extension, width, circumference, area, height e.g. of Dzambu-ling Dzl., of the Sumeru Glr., of the lunar mansions or the zodiac Glr.; Kyon-yaṅs-pa (khyon yangs pa) a wide extent, Kyon-yaṅs sa-gźi (khyon yangs sa gzhi) all the wide world (earth); Kyon-sdom (khyon sdom) Cs. 1. narrow-extent. 2. sum, contents. -- Kyon-nas (khyon nas) thoroughly, Kyon-nas mi sdig-can (khyon nas mi sdig can) thoroughly a sinner; Kyon-nas med (khyon nas med) not at all C.
1. oblique, awry, irregularly shaped. -- 2. v. k̥yom-pa ('khyom pa).
(Cs. Kyor-pa (khyor pa)) as much as fills the hollow of the hand, a handful, cf. skyor (skyor); Kyor gaṅ (khyor gang), Kyor re (khyor re) one handful, Kyor do (khyor do) two handfuls.
v. K̥yol-ba ('khyol ba); Kyos-ma (khyos ma) C. = skyos-ma (skyos ma), skyas-ma (skyas ma).
1. a small bird of prey, sparrow-hawk, falcon, used for hunting, also bya-kra (bya khra); Kra-zur (khra zur) Sch.: a species of eagles; Kra-pa (khra pa) falconer. -- 2. v. the following article.
